---
title: "Raroc modeling across regions"
Paper: Financing Road Freight Electrification: Modeling the Total Cost of Ownership of Battery-Electric Trucks under Residual Value and Interest Rate Uncertainties.
Authors: Juan C. Lopez, Andreas Kopf, Matteo Craglia, and Miguel Jaller
---

#Loading packages
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(reshape)
library(ggplot2)
library(nlme)
library(Hmisc)
library(tidyverse)
library(stats)
library(scales)
library(FinCal)
library(waterfalls)
library(readxl)

```

#Basic setup
```{r}
# Suppress summarise info
options(dplyr.summarise.inform = FALSE)
options(tidyverse.quiet = TRUE)

ITFcolours<- c(
  "#1f77b4",  # strong blue
  "#d62728",  # bold red
  "#5fa00a",  # strong green
  "#ff7f0e",  # vivid orange
  "#9867dd",  # medium purple
  "#aaa000",  # lime yellow
  "#081ed3",  # turquoise
  "#390b79",  # deep indigo
  "#af1493",  # deep pink
  "#ff1147",  # tomato red
  "#2A6B1A",  # forest green
  "#ffcc00",  # bright yellow
  "#19998f",  # teal green
  "#e377c2",  # vibrant pink
  "#9f4500"   # orange-red
)

# Working directory: anchored on this Rmd's folder (portable across machines)
get_script_dir <- function() {
  ki <- try(knitr::current_input(dir = TRUE), silent = TRUE)
  if (!inherits(ki, "try-error") && !is.null(ki) && nzchar(ki)) return(dirname(ki))
  if (requireNamespace("rstudioapi", quietly = TRUE) && rstudioapi::isAvailable()) {
    p <- rstudioapi::getSourceEditorContext()$path
    if (nzchar(p)) return(dirname(p))
  }
  getwd()
}
script_dir <- get_script_dir()
```

#Preparing the input data
```{r}
input_dir   <- file.path(script_dir, "Inputs")
raroc_input <- file.path(script_dir, "Inputs", "RAROC")
output_dir  <- script_dir
```

```{r}
#Other inputs from https://theicct.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/tco-bets-europe-1-nov21.pdf

#General for NPV:
ownership_time <- 5 #years
discount_rate <- 0.095
interest_rate <- 0.06
upfront_cost <- 0 #Variable added for sensitivity analysis
residual_value <- 0.3 #Diesel
subsidy <- 0 #Subsidy

#Fixed costs:
registration_fee<- 800 #[Eur] // Value for France
DT_price <- data.frame( #[Eur]
  year = c(2020, 2030),
  price = c(133000, 145000)
)
BET_price <- read.csv(file.path(input_dir, "BET-Price.csv")) %>% #Source: https://theicct.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/tco-bets-europe-1-nov21.pdf
  dplyr::rename(year = Year, price = BET.Retail.Price..Eur.)
  
#Variable costs or parameters:
ownership_tax <- 950 #[Eur/year] // Value for France
road_use_charge <- 0 #[Eur/year] // Value for France
road_tolls <- 0.32 #[Eur/km] // Value for France
maintenance_costs <- data.frame( #[Eur/100km]
  fuel = c("diesel", "BET"),
  Maintenance_costs = c(18.5, 13.24)
)

diesel_consumption <- data.frame( #[l/100km]
  year = c(2020, 2030),
  fuel_consumption = c(30.7, 23.2)
)

energy_consumption <- data.frame( #[kWh/km]
  year = c(2020, 2030),
  energy_consumption = c(1.38, 0.99)
  )

diesel_price <- data.frame( #[Eur/l]
  year= c(2020,2030),
  price = c(0.905, 0.905)
)

electricity_price <- data.frame( #[Eur/kWh]
  year = c(2020, 2030),
  price = c(0.369, 0.127)
)

VKT_year <- read.csv(file.path(input_dir, "VKT_perYear.csv")) %>% #Source: https://theicct.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/11/tco-bets-europe-1-nov21.pdf
  dplyr::rename(service_year = Service.year, VKT = VKT..km.)
```


#Interpolating data between years

```{r}
years_to_interpolate = 2020:2035

DT_price <- approx(x=DT_price$year, y=DT_price$price, xout = 2020:2030, method = "linear", rule = 2) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::rename (year = x, price = y)
 
diesel_consumption <- approx(x=diesel_consumption$year, y=diesel_consumption$fuel_consumption, xout = years_to_interpolate, method = "linear", rule = 2) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::rename (year = x, fuel_consumption = y)

energy_consumption <- approx(x=energy_consumption$year, y=energy_consumption$energy_consumption, xout = years_to_interpolate, method = "linear", rule = 2) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::rename (year = x, energy_consumption = y)

diesel_price <- approx(x=diesel_price$year, y=diesel_price$price, xout = years_to_interpolate, method = "linear", rule = 2) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::rename (year = x, price = y)

electricity_price <- approx(x=electricity_price$year, y=electricity_price$price, xout = years_to_interpolate, method = "linear", rule = 2) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::rename (year = x, price = y)
```


##--------------- RAROC AND RoE ------------

```{r}
PD <- readRDS(file.path(raroc_input, "PD_WB.rds"))

recovery_rate <- readRDS(file.path(raroc_input, "RecoveryRate_WB.rds"))


CB <- readRDS(file.path(raroc_input, "CentralBank_WB.rds")) %>%
  mutate(Value = Value/100) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Region.wb))

OpCosts <- readRDS(file.path(raroc_input, "OperationalCost_WB.rds"))
```

#FUNCTION TO CALCULATE THE INTEREST NEEDED TO REACH CERTAIN LEVEL OF RETURN ON EQUITY

```{r}
## Followed methodology: https://roopya.money/introduction-to-loan-pricing-and-raroc-risk-adjusted-return-on-capital/
###OUTPUT: INTEREST RATE
get_interest_raroc <- function(Purchase_year, Fuel, upfront_cost, subsidy, residual_value, PD, region, company){
  
  if (company == "SME") {
    data_RR <- recovery_rate %>% 
      select(Region, RR_industrial) %>%
      dplyr::rename(RR = "RR_industrial")
  } else if (company == "industry"){
    data_RR <- recovery_rate %>% 
      select(Region, RR_SMEs) %>%
      dplyr::rename(RR = "RR_SMEs")
  } else if (company == "Market"){
    data_RR <- recovery_rate %>% 
      select(Region, RR_industrial) %>%
      dplyr::rename(RR = "RR_industrial")
  }
  
  if (Fuel == "Diesel") {
    vehicle_price <- DT_price$price[DT_price$year == Purchase_year]
    
    RecoveryRate <- data_RR$RR[data_RR$Region == region]
    
  } else if (Fuel == "BET") {
    vehicle_price <- BET_price$price[BET_price$year == Purchase_year]
    RecoveryRate <- residual_value
  }
  
  total_loan <- (1 - upfront_cost - subsidy) * vehicle_price 
  
  LGD <- 1 - RecoveryRate #Source: https://www.investopedia.com/terms/l/lossgivendefault.asp
  EL <- total_loan*PD*LGD #Expected loss
  
  Cost_capital <- CB$Value[CB$Region.wb == region]

  Economic_capital_requirement <- 0.08
  
  Economic_capital = Economic_capital_requirement*total_loan
  
  raroc <- (0.10/CB$Value[CB$Region.wb == "Europe"])*Cost_capital
  
  Op_expenses <- OpCosts$Value[OpCosts$Region.wb == region]

  NII <- (raroc*Economic_capital + EL)/(1-Op_expenses)
  
  interest_rate_output <- (NII + Cost_capital*Economic_capital)/total_loan
  
  return(interest_rate_output)
}
```

#CALCULATING ACROSS DIFFERENT PD AND RVG 

## Large companies
```{r}
regions <- PD$Region

interest_BET_region_industry <- data.frame()

for (region in regions) {
  PD_region <- PD$PD_industrial[PD$Region==region]
  
  interest <- get_interest_raroc(Purchase_year = 2025, Fuel="Diesel", upfront_cost = upfront_cost, subsidy = subsidy, residual_value = 0, PD = PD_region, region = region, company = "industry") #With fuel = "Diesel" I'm considering typical values for Recovery rates
  interest_BET_result <- get_interest_raroc(Purchase_year = 2025, Fuel="BET", upfront_cost = upfront_cost, subsidy = subsidy, residual_value = 0, PD = PD_region, region = region, company = "industry")
  interest_BET_high_pd <- get_interest_raroc(Purchase_year = 2025, Fuel="BET", upfront_cost = upfront_cost, subsidy = subsidy, residual_value = 0, PD = PD_region*1.5, region = region, company = "industry")
  
  interest_BET_region_industry <- rbind(interest_BET_region_industry, 
                                        data.frame(interest_BET = interest_BET_result, 
                                                   high_interest_BET = interest_BET_high_pd,
                                                   typical_interest = interest,
                                                   region = region))
  # }
}

```

```{r}
interest_long <- interest_BET_region_industry %>%
  mutate(bet_value = interest_BET) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(interest_BET, typical_interest, high_interest_BET),
               names_to = "rate_type",
               values_to = "interest_rate") %>%
  mutate(rate_type = recode(rate_type,
                            "interest_BET" = "Interest rate for BETs",
                            "typical_interest" = "Market Interest rate",
                            "high_interest_BET"= "Interest rate with high PD")) %>%
  mutate(rate_type = factor(rate_type,
                            levels = c("Interest rate for BETs",
                                       "Market Interest rate",
                                       "Interest rate with high PD"))) %>% 

  mutate(region = forcats::fct_reorder(region, bet_value, .desc = TRUE))
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_col(data = interest_long %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Market Interest rate"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate*100, fill = rate_type),
           position = position_nudge(y = -0.2),
           width = 0.4) +

  geom_col(data = interest_long %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate with high PD"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = rate_type),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0.2),
           width = 0.4,
           alpha = 0.2) + 

# BET rate (top of stack): plotted second so it's on top
  geom_col(data = interest_long %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate for BETs"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = rate_type),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0.2),
           width = 0.4) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Interest rate for BETs" = ITFcolours[1],
  "Interest rate with high PD" = ITFcolours[1],  # same color, will vary by alpha
  "Market Interest rate" = ITFcolours[2]
  ))+
  
  labs(title = "Interest rate requirements across regions - Large companies",
       x = "Interest rate [%]",
       y = "Region",
       fill = "") +
  theme_classic()

```

## SMEs
```{r}

interest_BET_region_SME <- data.frame()

for (region in regions) {
  PD_region <- PD$PD_SMEs[PD$Region==region]  
  
  interest <- get_interest_raroc(Purchase_year = 2025, Fuel="Diesel", upfront_cost = upfront_cost, subsidy = subsidy, residual_value = 0, PD = PD_region, region = region, company = "SME") #With fuel = "Diesel" I'm considering typical values for Recovery rates
  interest_BET_result <- get_interest_raroc(Purchase_year = 2025, Fuel="BET", upfront_cost = upfront_cost, subsidy = subsidy, residual_value = 0, PD = PD_region, region = region, company = "SME")
  interest_BET_high_pd <- get_interest_raroc(Purchase_year = 2025, Fuel="BET", upfront_cost = upfront_cost, subsidy = subsidy, residual_value = 0, PD = PD_region*1.5, region = region, company = "SME")
  
  
  interest_BET_region_SME <- rbind(interest_BET_region_SME, 
                                        data.frame(interest_BET = interest_BET_result, 
                                                   high_interest_BET = interest_BET_high_pd,
                                                   typical_interest = interest,
                                                   region = region))
}

```

```{r}
interest_long_SMEs <- interest_BET_region_SME %>%
  mutate(bet_value = interest_BET) %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = c(interest_BET, typical_interest, high_interest_BET),
               names_to = "rate_type",
               values_to = "interest_rate") %>%
  mutate(rate_type = recode(rate_type,
                            "interest_BET" = "Interest rate for BETs",
                            "typical_interest" = "Market Interest rate",
                            "high_interest_BET"= "Interest rate with high PD")) %>%
  mutate(rate_type = factor(rate_type,
                            levels = c("Interest rate for BETs",
                                       "Market Interest rate",
                                       "Interest rate with high PD"))) %>% 

  mutate(region = forcats::fct_reorder(region, bet_value, .desc = TRUE))
```

```{r}
ggplot() +
  geom_col(data = interest_long_SMEs %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Market Interest rate"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate*100, fill = rate_type),
           position = position_nudge(y = -0.2),
           width = 0.4) +

  geom_col(data = interest_long_SMEs %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate with high PD"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = rate_type),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0.2),
           width = 0.4,
           alpha = 0.2) + 

  geom_col(data = interest_long_SMEs %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate for BETs"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = rate_type),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0.2),
           width = 0.4) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Interest rate for BETs" = ITFcolours[1],
  "Interest rate with high PD" = ITFcolours[1],  # same color, will vary by alpha
  "Market Interest rate" = ITFcolours[2]
  ))+
  
  labs(title = "Interest rate requirements across regions - SMEs",
       x = "Interest rate [%]",
       y = "Region",
       fill = "") +
  theme_classic()

```
### Calculating regional market interest rates (with average PD and RR):

```{r}
interest_market <- data.frame()

for (region in regions) {
  PD_region <- PD$PD_industrial[PD$Region==region]  
  
  interest <- get_interest_raroc(Purchase_year = 2025, Fuel="Diesel", upfront_cost = upfront_cost, subsidy = subsidy, residual_value = 0, PD = PD_region, region = region, company = "Market") #With fuel = "Diesel" I'm considering typical values for Recovery rates
  
  interest_market <- rbind(interest_market, 
                           data.frame(typical_interest = interest,
                                      region = region))
}
```


### Plotting complete

```{r}
IR_regions <- ggplot() +

  ## Large fleets
  geom_col(data = interest_long %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate with high PD"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = "Large Fleets (BETs)", alpha = "High PD"),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0.2),
           width = 0.2) + 

  geom_col(data = interest_long %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate for BETs"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = "Large Fleets (BETs)", alpha = "Average PD"),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0.2),
           width = 0.2) +
  
  geom_col(data = interest_long_SMEs %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate with high PD"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = "SMEs (BETs)", alpha = "High PD"),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0),
           width = 0.2) + 

  geom_col(data = interest_long_SMEs %>%
             filter(rate_type == "Interest rate for BETs"),
           aes(y = region, x = interest_rate * 100, fill = "SMEs (BETs)", alpha = "Average PD"),
           position = position_nudge(y = 0),
           width = 0.2) +
  
  geom_col(data = interest_market,
           aes(y = region, x = typical_interest*100, fill = "Market rates (Industry)", alpha = "Average PD"),
           position = position_nudge(y = -0.2),
           width = 0.2) +
  
  scale_fill_manual(
    name = "Interest rate for:",
    values = c(
      "Large Fleets (BETs)" = ITFcolours[1],
      "SMEs (BETs)" = ITFcolours[2],  # same color, will vary by alpha
      "Market rates (Industry)" = ITFcolours[3]),
    breaks = c("Market rates (Industry)", "Large Fleets (BETs)", "SMEs (BETs)")  # order
  )+
  
  # Alpha legend
  scale_alpha_manual(
    name = "Risk level:",
    values = c("Average PD" = 1, "High PD" = 0.2)
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(expand=c(0,0)) +
  
  labs(#title = "Interest rate requirements across regions",
    title = NULL,
       x = "Interest rate (%)",
       y = "",
       fill = "") +
  theme_classic()

IR_regions
```
